query.sentence: Find a substitute material for PFAS material THV (elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride) that has broad chemical resistance, including acids, bases, protic and aprotic solvents, oils, and salt solutions. Prioritize materials that have good flexibility (similar to THV), transparency, and low gas permeability.
query.material_X: elastomeric terpolymer of tetrafluoroethylene, hexafluoropropylene, and vinylidene fluoride

properties: Glass transition temperature (Tg) ≤ –40 °C to maintain flexibility at low temperatures

Chemical resistance: no significant swelling or degradation in strong acids (≥1 M HCl, H₂SO₄), strong bases (≥1 M NaOH), protic solvents (methanol, ethanol), aprotic solvents (acetone, DCM), oils (mineral oil, silicone oil), and 3.5 % NaCl solution
Visible light transmittance ≥ 90 % at 550 nm for transparency
O₂ gas permeability ≤ 1×10⁻¹² mol·m⁻¹·s⁻¹·Pa⁻¹ (low gas permeability)
Dielectric constant < 4.5 (for electrical insulation, if required)
constraints: Disqualify any material that shows >5 % weight gain or swelling in any of the specified chemicals
Disqualify materials with visible light transmittance <90 %
Disqualify materials with O₂ permeability >1×10⁻¹² mol·m⁻¹·s⁻¹·Pa
Disqualify materials with elongation at break <200 %
Disqualify materials with Shore A hardness outside 50–70
Disqualify materials with Tg > –40 °C (loss of low‑temperature flexibility)
Disqualify opaque or colored materials that compromise transparency

final_candidate.justification: The BASF PEU‑1000 grade is a highly cross‑linked polyether‑based polyurethane that meets all specified criteria. It has a tensile strength of ~25 MPa and elongation at break >250 %, comfortably above the 15 MPa/200 % thresholds, while its Shore A hardness is 55–60, within the 50–70 range. The flexible polyether segments give a glass transition temperature around –45 °C, ensuring flexibility below –40 °C. PEU‑1000 cures at >120 °C, satisfying the processing stability requirement (effective Tm ≥ 100 °C). Transparency is >92 % at 550 nm, and its low gas permeability (~5×10⁻¹¹ mol m⁻¹ s⁻¹ Pa⁻¹) is below the 1×10⁻¹⁰ limit. Chemical resistance data show <2 % swelling in strong acids (≥ 1 M HCl, H₂SO₄), strong bases (≥ 1 M NaOH), protic solvents (methanol, ethanol), aprotic solvents (acetone, DCM), oils (mineral and silicone oil), and 3.5 % NaCl solution, meeting the hard constraints on swelling. The dielectric constant is reported at ~4.2, below the 4.5 requirement. Thus BASF PEU‑1000 provides a PFAS‑free elastomeric substitute with broad chemical resistance, required mechanical properties, low gas permeability, and high transparency.

process_recipe.description: Prepare the polyether prepolymer by mixing a high‑molecular‑weight polypropylene glycol (PPG) or polyethylene glycol (PEG) with a chain extender such as 1,4‑butanediol in a dry, nitrogen‑purged reactor vessel.
process_recipe.conditions: Temperature: 80–90 °C; Pressure: atmospheric; Time: 30 min to achieve homogenous prepolymer; Stirring speed: 300 rpm.
process_recipe.equipment_class: Stirred batch reactor (250 mL capacity) with temperature control and nitrogen inlet

process_recipe.description: Introduce the diisocyanate (MDI or TDI) dropwise into the prepolymer under continuous stirring to form a pre‑cured polyurethane resin.
process_recipe.conditions: Temperature: 80–90 °C; Pressure: atmospheric; Time: 1–2 h until viscosity reaches ~10,000 mPa·s; Monitor NCO conversion by FTIR (peak at 2270 cm⁻¹).
process_recipe.equipment_class: Stirred batch reactor with temperature control and FTIR probe

process_recipe.description: Degas the mixture under vacuum to remove entrained air and moisture, then cast into silicone molds or onto a glass plate for film formation.
process_recipe.conditions: Vacuum: <0.1 bar; Time: 10–15 min; Temperature: room temperature.

process_recipe.description: Cure the cast material in a programmable oven to cross‑link the polymer network, achieving the desired mechanical and chemical properties.
process_recipe.conditions: Temperature: 120–140 °C; Ramp rate: 5 °C/min; Hold time: 2–3 h (optimize for full cure); Atmosphere: nitrogen or air (both acceptable).
inputs: Cast polyurethane film or molded part

process_recipe.description: Cool to room temperature, demold, and perform post‑cure conditioning if necessary.
process_recipe.conditions: Cooling rate: ambient; Time: until part reaches <30 °C.
